macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: S&P 500 Posts Fifth Consecutive Weekly Loss Amid Middle East Tensions
items.summary: The S&P 500 fell 2% last week, marking its first five-straight weekly decline since 2022, as elevated oil prices and Middle East conflict uncertainty weighed on markets. The index is pacing for its worst month in a year.

items.title: Oil Prices Surge to $112 Per Barrel as Central Banks Face Inflation Pressure
items.summary: Brent crude climbed to $112 per barrel amid Middle East conflict, raising questions about whether central bank policy rates can keep pace with rising inflation. The economic shock is intensifying government focus on energy security.

items.title: US Treasury Yields Rise to 4.43% Amid Market Volatility
items.summary: U.S. 10-year Treasury yields climbed to 4.43% as market jitters intensified over geopolitical tensions and inflation concerns.

items.title: Central Banks Shift to Hawkish Stance as Inflation Concerns Mount
items.summary: Central bank rhetoric has taken a hawkish turn away from earlier expectations of rate cuts, with the Fed, Bank of England, and emerging market central banks reassessing policy amid elevated inflation pressures.

items.title: US Energy Exports Rise as Iran Blockade Shifts Global Supply
items.summary: The blockade of Iranian ports in the Strait of Hormuz is providing geopolitical leverage for U.S. oil exports, with President Trump declaring the U.S. will not allow Iran to blackmail the world.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Tops $74,000 as Crypto Market Cap Rises 4.53% to $2.52 Trillion
items.summary: The cryptocurrency market rallied with Bitcoin reaching $74,670 (up 5.48%), Ethereum up 7.6-9%, and top performers like Algorand (+9.4%) and Aptos (+6.2%). The surge is driven by returning risk appetite, $1.1 billion in ETP inflows, and easing geopolitical tensions.

items.title: Crypto Market Rallies on $1.1 Billion Inflows; Bitcoin Nears $75,000, Ethereum Above $2,300
items.summary: Bitcoin traded at $74,442 (up 4.92%) and Ethereum at $2,366 (up 7.92%), with altcoins like BNB, XRP, Solana, and Cardano surging up to 7.78%. The rebound is supported by massive ETP inflows—the highest since mid-January—and short covering.

items.title: Bitcoin Hits Four-Week High of $74,929 Amid Trump-Iran Peace Talk Signals
items.summary: Bitcoin rose to $74,929 and Ethereum to $2,378 (up 9%), as traders bet against escalation in Middle East tensions following Trump's comments on Iran peace talks. Bitcoin ETFs saw $833 million inflows last week, with Ethereum ETFs at $187 million.

items.title: BitMine Acquires 71,524 ETH for $156.4 Million, Reserves Exceed 4.8 Million ETH
items.summary: BitMine boosted its Ethereum holdings to over 4.8 million ETH (4.04% of supply) with a $156.4 million purchase. This reflects ongoing institutional accumulation amid market recovery.

items.title: Institutional Inflows Surge as Retail Exits; Fear and Greed Index Jumps to 21
items.summary: CoinShares reports significant fund inflows due to easing geopolitics and weak US CPI data; Bitcoin shorts saw largest inflow since November 2024. Experts note the cycle is now institutional-led.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Federal Officials and Bank CEOs Hold Emergency Meeting on Anthropic's Mythos AI Dangers
items.summary: Federal officials and bank CEOs are convening an emergency meeting this week due to concerns over Anthropic's latest AI model, Mythos, and its potentially destructive capabilities. The meeting highlights growing regulatory worries about advanced AI risks.

items.title: Anthropic Releases Claude Managed Agents for Enterprise Workflows
items.summary: Anthropic has launched Claude Managed Agents, a new tool aimed at enterprise users for managing AI agent deployments, with explanations and demos clarifying its features and target audience.

items.title: NVIDIA Offers Free Access to MiniMax M2.7 Model on Build Platform
items.summary: NVIDIA has made the new MiniMax M2.7, a 230B parameter sparse MoE model with 204.8K context window, available as a free endpoint on build.nvidia.com, optimized for coding, reasoning, and agentic tasks.

items.title: OpenClaw 4.12 Update Introduces Active Memory for Persistent AI Agents
items.summary: OpenClaw 4.12 addresses AI agent forgetfulness with Active Memory, enabling automatic context retrieval from past interactions, plus LM Studio support, security fixes, and improved policies.

items.title: Attempted Murder Charges Filed in Attack on OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's Home
items.summary: Authorities have filed attempted murder charges related to an attack on OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's home, marking a significant security incident in the AI industry.

items.title: 100-Hour Test Shows Claude Code Edges Out Antigravity in AI Coding Tools
items.summary: A comprehensive 100-hour comparison reveals Claude Code outperforming Google Antigravity in real-world coding tasks, with Anthropic reporting 50% productivity gains despite past token cost issues.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Windrose Launches in Early Access with Multiple Biomes
items.summary: Windrose, a promising survival pirate game, launched in early access on April 14th featuring three biomes including two new areas, with a fourth biome planned soon. Console releases are in development after its PC debut.

items.title: Disney and Epic Games Developing New Extraction Shooter
items.summary: Disney and Epic are working on a new extraction shooter that has received positive internal reviews, amid discussions of other projects facing mixed feedback.

items.title: GTA6 Reportedly Hacked
items.summary: Grand Theft Auto 6 has been hacked, sparking major discussions in the gaming news cycle alongside other updates.

items.title: Amazon's Luna Gaming Service Shuts Down
items.summary: Amazon's Luna cloud gaming service has closed, marking another setback in the cloud gaming space.

items.title: Samson Game Launches as Buggy Disaster
items.summary: New release Samson is facing severe criticism for bugs, with developers now addressing issues post-launch.

items.title: The Cube Save Us Extraction Shooter Shuts Down
items.summary: Extraction shooter The Cube Save Us has shut down shortly after release, continuing a trend of failures in the genre.

items.title: FAA Recruits Video Gamers for Air Traffic Controllers
items.summary: The Federal Aviation Administration is targeting video gamers to address a shortage of air traffic controllers, leveraging their quick reflexes and skills.

items.title: Rumors of New Metro Game Surface
items.summary: Speculation about a new Metro game has emerged in recent gaming news discussions.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.
